questions: Should X-Helix-Org-ID be sent for all requests or only hxi_ key requests?
How should hlx org switch behave differently for hxi_ keys vs JWT tokens?
How should hlx org list mark the current org for hxi_ key auth?
What happens when hlx org switch is called before orgId is set in config (bootstrap)?
How should ambiguous org names be handled in hlx org switch?

answers.answer: Only send X-Helix-Org-ID when config.apiKey.startsWith('hxi_') AND config.orgId is set. JWT tokens embed their org in the token payload and use the server-side POST /api/auth/switch-org flow. Sending the header for JWTs would be ignored and could confuse debugging.
evidence: http.ts:52-57 — auth header branch already distinguishes hxi_ from JWT tokens
Ticket: 'CLI requests must send the selected org to the server' and 'Use one explicit request header for selected org'
Ticket: 'Existing session JWT behavior may continue calling /api/auth/switch-org'

answers.answer: For hxi_ keys: resolve org name via GET /api/auth/me, save orgId/orgName to local config only, preserve apiKey unchanged. For JWT tokens: keep existing behavior (call POST /api/auth/switch-org, save new accessToken). The branch point is config.apiKey.startsWith('hxi_').
evidence: switch.ts:43-56 — current code calls POST /api/auth/switch-org and replaces apiKey with JWT
Ticket: 'hlx org switch <org-name-or-id> with an hxi_ key must update local ~/.hlx/config.json only'
Ticket: 'hlx org switch must not call /api/auth/switch-org for hxi_ keys'

answers.answer: For hxi_ keys, mark current org by comparing org.id === config.orgId (local config state). For JWT tokens, keep existing behavior using org.id === data.organization.id (server state). If no orgId is in config, no org is marked as current.
evidence: list.ts:20 — current code marks by data.organization.id
config.ts:15-16 — HxConfig has optional orgId field
Ticket: 'hlx org list must show all available orgs and mark the locally selected org'

answers.answer: No special CLI handling needed. The server's /api/auth/me endpoint works for API key auth even without X-Helix-Org-ID (bootstrap exemption). The CLI gets back availableOrganizations and can resolve the org name. After saving orgId to config, subsequent requests include the header.
evidence: Server routes/api.ts:168 — /auth/me is before the global requireAuth gate at L239
Server tech-research: resolveApiKeyAuth builds minimal AuthContext for /auth/me when header missing
switch.ts:27 — cmdOrgSwitch calls GET /api/auth/me for name resolution

answers.answer: If the org name matches multiple entries in availableOrganizations (case-insensitive), print all matching orgs with their IDs and exit with a clear error. Change from .find() to .filter() and check length > 1.
evidence: Ticket: 'For ambiguous org names, fail with a clear error instead of selecting the first match'
switch.ts:28-29 — current code uses .find() which returns first match

statement: The CLI changes are straightforward conditional branches on key type in three files: http.ts adds X-Helix-Org-ID header for hxi_ keys, switch.ts does local-only config update for hxi_ keys, and list.ts marks current org by config.orgId for hxi_ keys. JWT behavior remains unchanged in all cases. No new files, no new dependencies, no architectural changes needed.
